author: @aion2177
text:
what is the principle behind this phenomena? Thermal shock? Some intense corrosion?

Can it be used to cut a closed shape like a circle? What happens with the glass when a closed shape is present? Hmm my guess is that it will just crack in long breaks and not rely work - for examle, lets say that you want to take out a complex shape - say a cog or a 5 pointed star out of glass.. Would be super awesome if you can make any shape of glass :D 

Also can be used for a 3D block of  glass? To try something like sculpting - you put a bit of ink you remove a bit of material easily, then you put some more ink , you remove some more material.. like in layers of 0.3 cm deep - so on.

Im thinking, if this principle is something to do with thermal shock then will most likely be superficial. Make the hard material superficially soft and easy grind-able.

How deep the crack effect can be?

Also why only glass? - what about marble, granite? - maybe we figure out how insane blocks like the megalithic ones from Peru and Egypt have been processed.. how they were able to scoop so easily the rock .. they had no microwave but who knows :))  but idk that sounds too crazy :)) 

I wish i had that ink to try it on few more materials :)

author: @DanielbenDaniel
text: We just painted a strip on plexiglass popped it in microwave wave  for 10 seconds then bent plexi in half.   One end that was not covered with ink very well started on fire. But it worked!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: with plexi it would probably work well if you put a battery across it - resistance heater?
